ria). The tested derivatives were showed varying
antibacterial activities as showing in Table 1. Compound
(6d) showed the highest effect against all tested bacteria
among the other compounds. This might be due to the
pyrimidinyl group that is a highly efficient
pharmacophore. We can conclude that introduction of
pyrimidinyl group into N-(4-substituted sulfamoyl phe-
nyl)-1H-benzo[d]imidazole-2-carbo hydrazonoyl cyanide
may enhance the antibacterial activity. This possibility
was confirmed by displaying compound (6g) high
antibacterial activity against S. epidermidis, which has
4-methyl 2-pyrimidinyl group. In the same time, com-
pound (6b) showed high activity against E. coli, which
has a NH CH NH group. The other compounds did
2
not show any activities against tested bacteria. In addi-
tion,compound(6d)wasstudiedforminimuminhibitory
concentration (MIC), where it showed 312.5, 156.25,
625, and 1,250μg/ml forS. aureus, S.epidermidis,E. coli,
and K. pneumonia, respectively. In previous work,[28] the
